info.title: Model Control Plane API
info.description:
# Overview

This API provides endpoints for the Model Control Plane API in the Agentic AI Platform.

## Getting Started

PRIOR TO USING THIS API, YOU MUST AGREE TO OUR TERMS OF SERVICE

The API is protected by OAuth2.0 and requires a valid TID access token in the Authorization header. Refer to [TID Authentication Guide](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/authentication) for details. Please refer e.g. to [Trimble Connect Getting Started Guide](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/connect/getting_started) for the detailed steps needed to interact with the APIs.

## Required TID Token Scopes

The Model Service API requires the following TID token scopes:
* openid - Get user identity
* models - Use model workflow execution endpoints
* profile - Get user profile information

## Capabilities Overview

* Manage AI models
    * Get model configurations
    * Get model health
    * Get model gateway connectivity
* You can filter model list by model provider and model status
* Advanced filtering using FIQL (Feed Item Query Language) for complex queries

## Common API Concepts

In most cases, this API uses HTTPS requests with JSON-formatted content in requests and responses. The chat API uses SSE response streaming because of the asynchronous and long-running nature of this API.

## Cross-Origin Resource Sharing

This API features Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) implemented in compliance with the [W3C spec](https://www.w3.org/TR/cors/) that allows cross-domain communication from the browser. All responses have a wildcard same-origin which makes them completely public and accessible to everyone, including any code on any site.

## Date Format

All dates in the API use UTC and are strings in the [ISO 8601 combined date and time representation\nformat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Combined_date_and_time_representations). Example: 2015-05-15T15:50:38.311Z.

## Eventual Read Consistency

The API responses are eventually consistent. When you read data from the service, the response might not reflect the results of the most recently completed write operation. The response might include some stale data due to caching in place. If you repeat your read request after a short time, the response should return the latest data.

## Paginated Responses

Some endpoints that return collection of resources follow the same schema for query parameters and response structure to paginate the results. Pagination allows to limit the size of the large responses and is aimed to improve the responsiveness and overall performance when fetching large result sets. The overall latency is improved by parallelization of data processing and transfer.

Pagination is cursor-based and client-driven. The requests accept pageIndex and pageSize query parameters.

The pageSize parameter informs the server on how many items the client wants to receive in a single page at most. Server may return fewer items than requested if the requested response payload is too large or if some of the items are not accessible to the requester. If pageSize parameter is not specified then the server returns the maximum possible number of items by default.

The maximum page size is different for each endpoint and specified as part of the endpoint description. It may also vary depending on the request parameters (e.g. fields query parameter). If the pageSize parameter is greater than the specified limit the endpoint can support, the server will return an error to help the client avoid bugs in pagination implementation.
The links.next.href property in the response contains the URL to the next page of results. If there are no more results, the links.next.href property is not present in the response. Server may return a response with links.next.href property even if the response contains no or fewer than requested (pageSize) items. Server may return fewer items than pageSize requested (or even zero). This could happen e.g. if resources requested are too large to fit into a single response or if some (or all pageSize requested) resources are not accessible to the requester. In this case, the requester is expected to call links.next.href to fetch the rest of the resources, if links.next.href is present in the response. In extreme cases, the response could be empty with the links.next.href present, which means that all resources on the current page are not accessible to the requester.
The links.next.href property is always returned (even if there are no more results available) in certain APIs like Changes API where clients might want to persist the links.next.href after all current changes are fetched and use it to fetch new changes later.

## Common Query Parameters

In addition to query parameters (pageSize, skipToken) used for paging responses, there are several query parameters that are commonly used across all APIs.
* deleted - This query parameter is used to work with resources marked as "deleted". The default APIs (except APIs that access specific versions) behavior is to respond with 404 error when a resource marked as deleted is requested (or don't return deleted resource as part of the listing/search API). If this parameter is specified then the deletion marker will be ignored and all resources are returned.
* The fields query parameter is used to specify the fields to be included in the response. If not provided, a minimal number of fields will be included as default. The fields parameter is an array of field names, e.g. fields=name,description. The list of supported fields is defined for each resource type.
* The sortBy query parameter is used to specify the order of the items in the response. If not provided, the default order is applied (typically creation timestamp). The sortBy parameter is a string that can be one of the predefined values, e.g. createdAt, -createdAt:desc. The - and + prefixes are used to specify the order direction (ascending or descending). Default order is ascending if no prefix is specified.
* The search query parameter is used to filter the items in the response. If specified, only items that match the search criteria are returned. The search criteria is a substring that is matched against the specific fields of the resource.
* All identifiers are represented as strings, client should not make assumptions about the format of the identifiers, they are opaque to the client.
* All APIs return x-request-id header that contains a unique identifier for the request. This identifier can be used to track the request in the logs and for debugging purposes.

## Advanced Filtering with FIQL

The Model Service API supports FIQL (Feed Item Query Language) for advanced filtering capabilities on model listings. FIQL provides a powerful and flexible way to construct complex queries using a simple, URL-friendly syntax.

### FIQL Syntax

FIQL uses the following operators:

- **Comparison Operators:**
  - `==` : Equal to
  - `!=` : Not equal to
  - `=lt=` or `<` : Less than
  - `=le=` or `<=` : Less than or equal to
  - `=gt=` or `>` : Greater than
  - `=ge=` or `>=` : Greater than or equal to
  - `=in=` : Value is in the specified list
  - `=out=` : Value is not in the specified list

- **Logical Operators:**
  - `;` : AND operation
  - `,` : OR operation
  - `()` : Grouping for complex expressions

### Field Mappings

The following API field names are mapped to database fields for FIQL queries:

| API Field | Database Field | Description |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| `provider` | `modelProvider` | Model provider (e.g., openai, anthropic) |
| `contextWindow` | `tokenLimits.contextWindow` | Maximum context window size |
| `maxOutputTokens` | `tokenLimits.maxOutputTokens` | Maximum output tokens |
| `inputModalities` | `modalities.input` | Supported input modalities |
| `outputModalities` | `modalities.output` | Supported output modalities |
| `auth` | `authenticationSupported` | Authentication support |

### FIQL Query Examples

**Basic Filtering:**
```
provider==openai                    # OpenAI models only
contextWindow>100000                 # Models with large context windows
auth==true                          # Models with authentication support
```

**Complex AND Queries:**
```
provider==openai;contextWindow>100000              # OpenAI models with large context
provider==anthropic;maxOutputTokens>=4096          # Anthropic models with high output limits
```

**OR Queries:**
```
provider==openai,provider==anthropic               # OpenAI OR Anthropic models
contextWindow=lt=50000,contextWindow=gt=200000      # Small OR very large context windows
```

**Array Queries:**
```
inputModalities=in=(text,image)                     # Models supporting text and/or image input
outputModalities=out=(audio,video)                  # Models not supporting audio/video output
```

**Complex Grouped Queries:**
```
(provider==openai,provider==anthropic);contextWindow>50000
# OpenAI OR Anthropic models with large context windows

provider==openai;(contextWindow>100000,maxOutputTokens>4000)
# OpenAI models with either large context OR high output limits
```

### Using FIQL in API Requests

Add the `q` parameter to your model listing requests:

```
GET /v1/models?q=provider==openai;contextWindow>100000
GET /v1/models?q=(provider==openai,provider==anthropic);auth==true
```

**Note:** When using FIQL queries (`q` parameter), individual filter parameters (`status`, `provider`) are ignored to avoid conflicts. Use FIQL syntax to specify all desired filters.

## Soft and Hard Delete, Data Retention

## Handling Errors and Retry Logic

Common HTTP Response Status Codes are used. The response body for all error responses are in the same format and include the error code and message.

When you send requests to and get responses from the Service API, you might encounter two types of API errors:
* Client errors - Client errors are indicated by a 4xx HTTP response code. Client errors indicate that the service found a problem with the client request. This can include such things as an authentication failure or missing required parameters. Fix the issue in the client application before submitting the request again.
* Server errors - Server errors are indicated by a 5xx HTTP response code and need to be resolved by Trimble. You may resubmit/retry the request until it succeeds.
For each API error, the API Gateway returns the following values:
* A status code, for example, 400
* An error type, for example, tag:ValidationException
* An error message, for example, Supplied AttributeValue is empty, must contain exactly one of the supported data types

## Error Response Body Schema

All error responses follow the same structure for the response body and are conformant to [RFC 9457](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html) specification.
The error types use `tag:` URI schema and cannot be dereferenced.

| Error type                                | HTTP Status Code | Error description |
| ----------------------------------------- | --- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `tag:BadGateway`                          | 502 | The gateway received an invalid response from the upstream server.|
| `tag:BadModelServiceConfig`               | 400 | Bad model service configuration.|
| `tag:Conflict`                            | 409 | The request conflicts with the current state of the resource.|
| `tag:ConflictingHeaders`                  | 400 | Only one of If-Match or If-None-Match header is allowed in a request.|
| `tag:DatabaseOperationFailed`             | 500 | Database operation failed.|
| `tag:HttpError`                           | 500 | An HTTP error occurred.|
| `tag:InternalServerError`                 | 500 | The internal server error, please try again later.|
| `tag:InvalidAcl`                          | 400 | The ACL cannot be empty on space level.|
| `tag:InvalidIfMatchHeader`                | 400 | Invalid If-Match Header.|
| `tag:InvalidModel`                        | 400 | The specified model is invalid or not supported.|
| `tag:InvalidName`                         | 400 | The resource name is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidPageSize`                     | 400 | The request contains invalid page size.|
| `tag:InvalidParentParam`                  | 400 | The parent param is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidPayload`                      | 400 | One of id or name is mandatory. In case of a new file upload name is mandatory.|
| `tag:InvalidQueryParam`                   | 400 | Invalid query param: X.|
| `tag:InvalidSkipToken`                    | 400 | The request contains invalid skip token.|
| `tag:InvalidSortParameter`                | 400 | The given sort parameter is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidStateParam`                   | 400 | The state param is invalid.|
| `tag:InvalidUpload`                       | 400 | The file status is not UPLOADABLE.|
| `tag:InvalidUrl`                          | 404 | The url is not valid.|
| `tag:InvalidVersion`                      | 412 | Specified version is not the latest version.|
| `tag:InvalidParam`                        | 400 | Please provide a valid version.|
| `tag:MethodNotAllowed`                    | 405 | Method is not allowed on this resource.|
| `tag:NotAcceptableMediaType`              | 406 | The requested media type is not supported.|
| `tag:PageSizeExceeded`                    | 400 | The pageSize parameter exceeds the maximum supported page size (N).|
| `tag:PermissionDenied`                    | 403 | Permission denied for the requested resource.|
| `tag:ResourceNotFound`                    | 404 | The file or folder not found.|
| `tag:ServiceUnavailable`                  | 503 | The service is currently unavailable.|
| `tag:StaleObject`                         | 400 | Cannot update as request contains stale data.|
| `tag:TokenExchangeError`                  | 400 | Token exchange failed.|
| `tag:UnAuthorized`                        | 401 | The request is not authorized.|
| `tag:UnprocessableContent`                | 422 | The request contains invalid data.|

## Error Status Codes (Client and Server Errors)

HTTP status codes indicate whether an operation is successful or not.
A response code of 2xx indicates that the operation was successful. Other error codes indicate either a
client error (4xx) or a server error (5xx).
The error table lists the errors returned by the service. Some errors are resolved if you retry the same request.
As a general rule, the timeout errors, network errors, throttling errors, and 5xx errors may be retried.
The 401 error code usually indicates expired credentials and should be retried after refreshing the access token.
The error table indicates which errors are likely to be resolved with successive retries.

If the value of the Retry column is:

- **Yes** - Submit the same request again.
- **Yes if idempotent** - Submit the same request again, but only if the given method has been
implemented with idempotence.
- **No** - Fix the problem on the client-side before submitting a new request.

For more information about retrying requests, see Error Retries and Exponential Backoff.

| HTTP Status Code | Error type class                      | Retry                                             |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| 400              |  Bad Request Exception                | No                                                |
| 401              |  Unauthorized Exception               | No, could be retried after acquiring a new token  |
| 403              |  Access Denied Exception              | No                                                |
| 404              |  Not Found Exception                  | No                                                |
| 406              |  Not Acceptable Exception             | No                                                |
| 409              |  Conflict Exception                   | No                                                |
| 429              |  Too Many Requests Exception          | Yes                                               |
| 500              |  Internal Service Failure Exception   | Yes                                               |
| 502              |  Bad Gateway Exception                | Yes if idempotent                                 |
| 503              |  Service Unavailable Exception        | Yes                                               |
| 504              |  Endpoint Request Timed-out Exception | Yes if idempotent                                 |

## Catching Errors

For your application to run smoothly, you need to build in logic to catch and respond to errors.
One typical approach is to implement your request within a try block or if-then statement.
The client-side SDKs typically perform retries and error checking out of the box.

The error code and description are always sent in the request response body in addition to HTTP status code.
To troubleshoot the problem, please capture the request identifier using the `x-request-id`, `X-Azure-Ref`, `x-amzn-RequestId` HTTP response headers. Inform the value received in these headers to the support team.

## Error Retries and Exponential Backoff

Numerous components on a network, such as DNS servers, switches, load balancers,
and others can generate errors anywhere in the life of a given request.

The usual technique for dealing with these error responses in a networked environment
is to implement retries in the client application.
This technique increases the reliability of the application and reduces operational costs for the developer.

You should retry original requests that receive server errors (5xx).
However, client errors (4xx, other than a TooManyRequestsException) indicate you need to
revise the request itself to correct the problem before trying again.

In addition to simple retries, it is recommended to use an exponential backoff algorithm for better flow control.
The idea behind exponential backoff is to use progressively longer waits between retries
for consecutive error responses.
For example, you might let one second elapse
before the first retry, four seconds before the second retry, 16 seconds before the third retry, and so on.
However, if the request has not succeeded after a minute, the problem might be a hard limit and not the
request rate.
For example, you may have reached the maximum number of pipelines allowed. Set the maximum
number of retries to stop around one minute.

get.description: Lists available models with pagination support.

parameters.name: pageSize

schema.description: Number of results per page (1-100, default: 20).
schema.title: Pagesize

parameters.description: Number of results per page (1-100, default: 20).

parameters.name: fields

schema.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include in response.
schema.title: Fields

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include in response.

parameters.name: sortBy

schema.description: Field to sort by. Use '-' prefix for descending (e.g., '-modelId').
schema.title: Sortby

parameters.description: Field to sort by. Use '-' prefix for descending (e.g., '-modelId').

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Search term to filter models by name or description.
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Search term to filter models by name or description.

parameters.name: deleted

schema.description: Include deleted models in response (default: false).
schema.title: Deleted

parameters.description: Include deleted models in response (default: false).

parameters.name: q

schema.description: FIQL (Feed Item Query Language) query for advanced filtering. Supports operators: ==, !=, <, <=, >, >=, =in=, =out=. Use ';' for AND, ',' for OR, '()' for grouping. Example: 'provider==openai;contextWindow>100000'
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: FIQL (Feed Item Query Language) query for advanced filtering. Supports operators: ==, !=, <, <=, >, >=, =in=, =out=. Use ';' for AND, ',' for OR, '()' for grouping. Example: 'provider==openai;contextWindow>100000'

get.description: Checks basic connectivity to the model gateway.

schema.title: Response Check Gateway Connectivity V1 Models Gateway Connectivity Get

url.title: Url
url.description: Gateway endpoint URL that was called

body.title: Body
body.description: Request body sent to the gateway

messages.content: Hello! This is a health check.

GatewayRequest.title: GatewayRequest
GatewayRequest.description: Gateway request details for health check.

statusCode.title: Statuscode
statusCode.description: HTTP status code returned by the gateway

body.title: Body
body.description: Response body from the gateway (JSON object or error text)

GatewayResponse.title: GatewayResponse
GatewayResponse.description: Gateway response details for health check.

maxItemsPerRequest.title: Maxitemsperrequest
maxItemsPerRequest.description: Maximum number of items of this modality per request

maxSizeBytesPerItem.title: Maxsizebytesperitem
maxSizeBytesPerItem.description: Maximum size in bytes per item

supportedFormats.title: Supportedformats
supportedFormats.description: Allowed image MIME types for model input

ImageConstraint.title: ImageConstraint
ImageConstraint.description: Image-specific input constraints (extends base for future fields).

image.description: Constraints when image is accepted as model input

maxTotalSizeBytes.title: Maxtotalsizebytes
maxTotalSizeBytes.description: Maximum total request payload size in bytes across all modalities

InputConstraints.title: InputConstraints
InputConstraints.description: Optional per-modality input limits; extend with pdf/audio/video as needed.

InputModality.title: InputModality
InputModality.description: Allowed model input modality values (catalog / seed).

input.title: Input
input.description: Supported input modalities (e.g., text, image, audio)

ModelCost.title: ModelCost
ModelCost.description:
Cost information for a model.

Supports both token-based pricing (for chat/completion models)
and request-based pricing (for image/video/audio generation models).

inputConstraints.description: Optional limits for multimodal inputs (per modality)

status.description: Derive API status from lifecycle.stage for backward compatibility.

inputConstraints.title: Inputconstraints
inputConstraints.description: Per-modality input limits (e.g. image max count, size, formats)

ModelDetailsResponse.title: ModelDetailsResponse
ModelDetailsResponse.description:
Response model for model details that supports field selection.

This model follows the same pattern as AgentResponse in the agent service:
- Core fields are required (always present)
- Extended fields are optional (present based on field selection)
- Uses response_model_exclude_none=True for partial responses

checkedAt.title: Checkedat
checkedAt.description: ISO timestamp when the health check was performed

errorMessage.title: Errormessage
errorMessage.description: Error message if the health check failed

gatewayRequest.description: Details of the request sent to the gateway

gatewayResponse.description: Details of the response received from the gateway

ModelHealth.title: ModelHealth
ModelHealth.description: Operational status of a model endpoint.

minimumGaMonths.title: Minimumgamonths
minimumGaMonths.description: Policy setting, only when in source

ModelLifecycle.title: ModelLifecycle
ModelLifecycle.description:
Lifecycle information for a model.

Syncs with DB/seed data (model_details.json). Only includes fields present
in source; minimumGaMonths is optional and omitted when not in DB.

ModelLifecycleStage.title: ModelLifecycleStage
ModelLifecycleStage.description: Model lifecycle stages following Azure/Gemini patterns.

OutputModality.title: OutputModality
OutputModality.description: Allowed model output modality values (catalog / seed).

href.title: Href
href.description: URL to the page. Contains encoded skipToken for cursor-based pagination.

PageLink.title: PageLink
PageLink.description: Link to a page in paginated responses.

data.title: Data
data.description: Array of items in the current page.

moreItemsAvailable.title: Moreitemsavailable
moreItemsAvailable.description: Indicates if more items are available beyond the current page. Used in certain APIs where links.next.href is always returned.

totalCount.title: Totalcount
totalCount.description: Total number of items across all pages (optional, may not always be provided).

next.description: Link to the next page of results. Absent if no more results.

prev.description: Link to the previous page of results. Absent on first page.

self.description: Link to the current page of results.

pricePerImage.title: Priceperimage
pricePerImage.description: Price per image generated/edited in USD

RequestBasedPricing.title: RequestBasedPricing
RequestBasedPricing.description:
Request-based pricing for image generation models.

Pricing is expressed in USD per request/image.
Supports both flat-rate and tiered pricing models.
All fields are optional to handle various pricing structures.

price1mInputTokens.title: Price1Minputtokens
price1mInputTokens.description: Price per 1 million input tokens in USD

price1mOutputTokens.title: Price1Moutputtokens
price1mOutputTokens.description: Price per 1 million output tokens in USD

TokenBasedPricing.title: TokenBasedPricing
TokenBasedPricing.description:
Token-based pricing for chat/completion models.

Pricing is expressed in USD per 1 million tokens.
All fields are optional to handle cases where pricing is not available.

contextWindow.title: Contextwindow
contextWindow.description: Maximum context window size in tokens

OAuth2.description: OAuth2 with PKCE (no client secret required). Default client_id: local-dev-client
